english_name: The Epistle of Paul the Apostle to the Ephesians

verses.latin_vulgate: Estote ergo imitatores Dei, sicut filii carissimi,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: γίνεσθε οὖν μιμηταὶ τοῦ θεοῦ ὡς τέκνα ἀγαπητά
verses.king_james: Be ye therefore followers of God, as dear children;

verses.latin_vulgate: et ambulate in dilectione, sicut et Christus dilexit nos, et tradidit semetipsum pro nobis, oblationem et hostiam Deo in odorem suavitatis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ περιπατεῖτε ἐν ἀγάπῃ καθὼς καὶ ὁ χριστὸς ἠγάπησεν ἡμᾶς καὶ παρέδωκεν ἑαυτὸν ὑπὲρ ἡμῶν προσφορὰν καὶ θυσίαν τῷ θεῷ εἰς ὀσμὴν εὐωδίας
verses.king_james: And walk in love, as Christ also hath loved us, and hath given himself for us an offering and a sacrifice to God for a sweetsmelling savour.

verses.latin_vulgate: Fornicatio autem, et omnis immunditia, aut avaritia, nec nominetur in vobis, sicut decet sanctos:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πορνεία δὲ καὶ πᾶσα ἀκαθαρσία ἢ πλεονεξία μηδὲ ὀνομαζέσθω ἐν ὑμῖν καθὼς πρέπει ἁγίοις
verses.king_james: But fornication, and all uncleanness, or covetousness, let it not be once named among you, as becometh saints;

verses.latin_vulgate: aut turpitudo, aut stultiloquium, aut scurrilitas, quæ ad rem non pertinet: sed magis gratiarum actio.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ αἰσχρότης καὶ μωρολογία ἢ εὐτραπελία τὰ οὐκ ἀνήκοντα ἀλλὰ μᾶλλον εὐχαριστία
verses.king_james: Neither filthiness, nor foolish talking, nor jesting, which are not convenient: but rather giving of thanks.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hoc enim scitote intelligentes: quod omnis fornicator, aut immundus, aut avarus, quod est idolorum servitus, non habet hæreditatem in regno Christi et Dei.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τοῦτο γὰρ ἔστε γινώσκοντες ὅτι πᾶς πόρνος ἢ ἀκάθαρτος ἢ πλεονέκτης ὅς ἐστιν εἰδωλολάτρης οὐκ ἔχει κληρονομίαν ἐν τῇ βασιλείᾳ τοῦ χριστοῦ καὶ θεοῦ
verses.king_james: For this ye know, that no whoremonger, nor unclean person, nor covetous man, who is an idolater, hath any inheritance in the kingdom of Christ and of God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nemo vos seducat inanibus verbis: propter hæc enim venit ira Dei in filios diffidentiæ.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μηδεὶς ὑμᾶς ἀπατάτω κενοῖς λόγοις διὰ ταῦτα γὰρ ἔρχεται ἡ ὀργὴ τοῦ θεοῦ ἐπὶ τοὺς υἱοὺς τῆς ἀπειθείας
verses.king_james: Let no man deceive you with vain words: for because of these things cometh the wrath of God upon the children of disobedience.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nolite ergo effici participes eorum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μὴ οὖν γίνεσθε συμμέτοχοι αὐτῶν
verses.king_james: Be not ye therefore partakers with them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Eratis enim aliquando tenebræ: nunc autem lux in Domino. Ut filii lucis ambulate:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἦτε γάρ ποτε σκότος νῦν δὲ φῶς ἐν κυρίῳ ὡς τέκνα φωτὸς περιπατεῖτε
verses.king_james: For ye were sometimes darkness, but now *are ye* light in the Lord: walk as children of light:

verses.latin_vulgate: fructus enim lucis est in omni bonitate, et justitia, et veritate:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ γὰρ καρπὸς τοῦ πνεύματος ἐν πάσῃ ἀγαθωσύνῃ καὶ δικαιοσύνῃ καὶ ἀληθείᾳ
verses.king_james: (For the fruit of the Spirit *is* in all goodness and righteousness and truth;)

verses.latin_vulgate: probantes quid sit beneplacitum Deo:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: δοκιμάζοντες τί ἐστιν εὐάρεστον τῷ κυρίῳ
verses.king_james: Proving what is acceptable unto the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: et nolite communicare operibus infructuosis tenebrarum, magis autem redarguite.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ μὴ συγκοινωνεῖτε τοῖς ἔργοις τοῖς ἀκάρποις τοῦ σκότους μᾶλλον δὲ καὶ ἐλέγχετε
verses.king_james: And have no fellowship with the unfruitful works of darkness, but rather reprove *them*.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quæ enim in occulto fiunt ab ipsis, turpe est et dicere.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τὰ γὰρ κρυφῇ γινόμενα ὑπ᾽ αὐτῶν αἰσχρόν ἐστιν καὶ λέγειν
verses.king_james: For it is a shame even to speak of those things which are done of them in secret.

verses.latin_vulgate: Omnia autem, quæ arguuntur, a lumine manifestantur: omne enim, quod manifestatur, lumen est.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τὰ δὲ πάντα ἐλεγχόμενα ὑπὸ τοῦ φωτὸς φανεροῦται πᾶν γάρ τό φανερούμενον φῶς ἐστίν
verses.king_james: But all things that are reproved are made manifest by the light: for whatsoever doth make manifest is light.

verses.latin_vulgate: Propter quod dicit: Surge qui dormis, et exsurge a mortuis, et illuminabit te Christus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: διὸ λέγει ἕγειραι ὁ καθεύδων καὶ ἀνάστα ἐκ τῶν νεκρῶν καὶ ἐπιφαύσει σοι ὁ χριστός
verses.king_james: Wherefore he saith, Awake thou that sleepest, and arise from the dead, and Christ shall give thee light.

verses.latin_vulgate: Videte itaque, fratres, quomodo caute ambuletis: non quasi insipientes,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: βλέπετε οὖν πῶς ἀκριβῶς περιπατεῖτε μὴ ὡς ἄσοφοι ἀλλ᾽ ὡς σοφοί
verses.king_james: See then that ye walk circumspectly, not as fools, but as wise,

verses.latin_vulgate: sed ut sapientes: redimentes tempus, quoniam dies mali sunt.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐξαγοραζόμενοι τὸν καιρόν ὅτι αἱ ἡμέραι πονηραί εἰσιν
verses.king_james: Redeeming the time, because the days are evil.

verses.latin_vulgate: Propterea nolite fieri imprudentes, sed intelligentes quæ sit voluntas Dei.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: διὰ τοῦτο μὴ γίνεσθε ἄφρονες ἀλλὰ συνιέντες τί τὸ θέλημα τοῦ κυρίου
verses.king_james: Wherefore be ye not unwise, but understanding what the will of the Lord *is*.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et nolite inebriari vino, in quo est luxuria, sed implemini Spiritu Sancto,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ μὴ μεθύσκεσθε οἴνῳ ἐν ᾧ ἐστιν ἀσωτία ἀλλὰ πληροῦσθε ἐν πνεύματι
verses.king_james: And be not drunk with wine, wherein is excess; but be filled with the Spirit;

verses.latin_vulgate: loquentes vobismetipsis in psalmis, et hymnis, et canticis spiritualibus, cantantes et psallentes in cordibus vestris Domino,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λαλοῦντες ἑαυτοῖς ψαλμοῖς καὶ ὕμνοις καὶ ᾠδαῖς πνευματικαῖς ᾄδοντες καὶ ψάλλοντες ἐν τῇ καρδίᾳ ὑμῶν τῷ κυρίῳ
verses.king_james: Speaking to yourselves in psalms and hymns and spiritual songs, singing and making melody in your heart to the Lord;

verses.latin_vulgate: gratias agentes semper pro omnibus in nomine Domini nostri Jesu Christi Deo et Patri,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εὐχαριστοῦντες πάντοτε ὑπὲρ πάντων ἐν ὀνόματι τοῦ κυρίου ἡμῶν ἰησοῦ χριστοῦ τῷ θεῷ καὶ πατρί
verses.king_james: Giving thanks always for all things unto God and the Father in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ;

verses.latin_vulgate: subjecti invicem in timore Christi.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὑποτασσόμενοι ἀλλήλοις ἐν φόβῳ θεοῦ
verses.king_james: Submitting yourselves one to another in the fear of God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Mulieres viris suis subditæ sint, sicut Domino:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: αἱ γυναῖκες τοῖς ἰδίοις ἀνδράσιν ὑποτάσσεσθε ὡς τῷ κυρίῳ
verses.king_james: Wives, submit yourselves unto your own husbands, as unto the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: quoniam vir caput est mulieris, sicut Christus caput est Ecclesiæ: ipse, salvator corporis ejus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅτι ὁ ἀνήρ ἐστιν κεφαλὴ τῆς γυναικὸς ὡς καὶ ὁ χριστὸς κεφαλὴ τῆς ἐκκλησίας καὶ αὐτὸς ἐστίν σωτὴρ τοῦ σώματος
verses.king_james: For the husband is the head of the wife, even as Christ is the head of the church: and he is the saviour of the body.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sed sicut Ecclesia subjecta est Christo, ita et mulieres viris suis in omnibus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀλλ᾽ ὡσπερ ἡ ἐκκλησία ὑποτάσσεται τῷ χριστῷ οὕτως καὶ αἱ γυναῖκες τοῖς ἰδίοις ἀνδράσιν ἐν παντί
verses.king_james: Therefore as the church is subject unto Christ, so *let* the wives *be* to their own husbands in every thing.

verses.latin_vulgate: Viri, diligite uxores vestras, sicut et Christus dilexit Ecclesiam, et seipsum tradidit pro ea,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἱ ἄνδρες ἀγαπᾶτε τὰς γυναῖκας ἑαυτῶν καθὼς καὶ ὁ χριστὸς ἠγάπησεν τὴν ἐκκλησίαν καὶ ἑαυτὸν παρέδωκεν ὑπὲρ αὐτῆς
verses.king_james: Husbands, love your wives, even as Christ also loved the church, and gave himself for it;

verses.latin_vulgate: ut illam sanctificaret, mundans lavacro aquæ in verbo vitæ,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἵνα αὐτὴν ἁγιάσῃ καθαρίσας τῷ λουτρῷ τοῦ ὕδατος ἐν ῥήματι
verses.king_james: That he might sanctify and cleanse it with the washing of water by the word,

verses.latin_vulgate: ut exhiberet ipse sibi gloriosam Ecclesiam, non habentem maculam, aut rugam, aut aliquid hujusmodi, sed ut sit sancta et immaculata.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἵνα παραστήσῃ αὐτὴν ἑαυτῷ ἔνδοξον τὴν ἐκκλησίαν μὴ ἔχουσαν σπίλον ἢ ῥυτίδα ἤ τι τῶν τοιούτων ἀλλ᾽ ἵνα ᾖ ἁγία καὶ ἄμωμος
verses.king_james: That he might present it to himself a glorious church, not having spot, or wrinkle, or any such thing; but that it should be holy and without blemish.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ita et viri debent diligere uxores suas ut corpora sua. Qui suam uxorem diligit, seipsum diligit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὕτως ὀφείλουσιν οἱ ἄνδρες ἀγαπᾶν τὰς ἑαυτῶν γυναῖκας ὡς τὰ ἑαυτῶν σώματα ὁ ἀγαπῶν τὴν ἑαυτοῦ γυναῖκα ἑαυτὸν ἀγαπᾷ
verses.king_james: So ought men to love their wives as their own bodies. He that loveth his wife loveth himself.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nemo enim umquam carnem suam odio habuit: sed nutrit et fovet eam, sicut et Christus Ecclesiam:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐδεὶς γάρ ποτε τὴν ἑαυτοῦ σάρκα ἐμίσησεν ἀλλ᾽ ἐκτρέφει καὶ θάλπει αὐτήν καθὼς καὶ ὁ κύριος τὴν ἐκκλησίαν
verses.king_james: For no man ever yet hated his own flesh; but nourisheth and cherisheth it, even as the Lord the church:

verses.latin_vulgate: quia membra sumus corporis ejus, de carne ejus et de ossibus ejus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅτι μέλη ἐσμὲν τοῦ σώματος αὐτοῦ ἐκ τῆς σαρκός αὐτοῦ καί ἐκ τῶν ὀστέων αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: For we are members of his body, of his flesh, and of his bones.

verses.latin_vulgate: Propter hoc relinquet homo patrem et matrem suam, et adhærebit uxori suæ, et erunt duo in carne una.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀντὶ τούτου καταλείψει ἄνθρωπος τὸν πατέρα αὐτοῦ καὶ τὴν μητέρα καὶ προσκολληθήσεται πρὸς τὴν γυναῖκα αὐτοῦ καὶ ἔσονται οἱ δύο εἰς σάρκα μίαν
verses.king_james: For this cause shall a man leave his father and mother, and shall be joined unto his wife, and they two shall be one flesh.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sacramentum hoc magnum est, ego autem dico in Christo et in Ecclesia.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τὸ μυστήριον τοῦτο μέγα ἐστίν ἐγὼ δὲ λέγω εἰς χριστὸν καὶ εἰς τὴν ἐκκλησίαν
verses.king_james: This is a great mystery: but I speak concerning Christ and the church.

verses.latin_vulgate: Verumtamen et vos singuli, unusquisque uxorem suam sicut seipsum diligat: uxor autem timeat virum suum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πλὴν καὶ ὑμεῖς οἱ καθ᾽ ἕνα ἕκαστος τὴν ἑαυτοῦ γυναῖκα οὕτως ἀγαπάτω ὡς ἑαυτόν ἡ δὲ γυνὴ ἵνα φοβῆται τὸν ἄνδρα
verses.king_james: Nevertheless let every one of you in particular so love his wife even as himself; and the wife *see* that she reverence *her* husband.
